Loop 027

The autonomy-loop builder-reviewer loop

A two-worktree coding loop that pairs a builder with an independent adversarial reviewer and proves each new test can catch the change it protects.

Contributed by @inferencegod · Source

Ready-to-use prompt

Copy the loop

Use autonomy-loop for [task] in a repository with deterministic test, build, and lint gates. Run /autonomy-loop:autonomy-init once, configure the protected production branch, read-only control plane, builder and reviewer worktrees, maximum epochs, and no-progress limit, then approve the first task. In the builder terminal, run /autonomy-loop:builder followed by /loop 600. In the independent reviewer worktree, run /autonomy-loop:reviewer followed by /loop 600. Each wave, the builder reads LOOP-STATE.md, makes the smallest credible change, adds a falsifiable acceptance test that is red before the fix and green after it, runs the frozen-invariant gate, commits, and hands off. The reviewer independently reruns the gates, audits correctness, honesty, regressions, security, and UX, then proves the test by reverting or mutating the fix and requiring the test to fail. If the test stays green, the wave fails. Advance only when the deterministic gate and independent reviewer pass. Park for a human when an irreversible or protected path changes, an invariant drifts, the reviewer is unavailable, the trust tier is insufficient, failures repeat, or a budget is exhausted. Finish with the reviewed commit, gate evidence, test proof, trust tier, and remaining risks.

Verify / stop

Every accepted wave passes an independent proof-of-test gate.

The acceptance test fails without the change, passes with it, all configured gates pass, and protected production changes remain human- or forge-gated.

Context and guidance When to use it, steps, safety notes, and related loops
Published
Jun 19, 2026
Updated
Jun 19, 2026

Use this when

Use this for a repository task that benefits from continuous builder-reviewer handoffs and has strong deterministic gates plus real infrastructure-level branch protection.

How to run it

  1. Initialize the plugin, separate builder and reviewer worktrees, configure deterministic gates and frozen invariants, and verify the hardened production rails.
  2. Have the builder implement one bounded change with a red-before-green acceptance test, run the gates, commit, and update the git-backed baton.
  3. Have the reviewer rerun every gate, conduct the adversarial audit, and use revert-or-mutate proof to show that the new test catches the change.
  4. Accept and continue only on independent proof; otherwise return the findings to the builder or park for a human when a circuit breaker fires.

Why it works

The separate worktree and reviewer reduce self-approval, while the revert-or-mutate check catches tests that execute code without proving the fix. A committed baton makes the loop resumable after process or context loss.

Implementation note

Treat local hooks as tripwires, not a security boundary. Review and pin the plugin version, use server-enforced branch protection and separate principals for unattended promotion, and expect two continuously running agents to consume substantial tokens.

Related loops

The Clodex adversarial-review loop The Loop Harness verification loop
